report_name: China-Based Silver Fox APT Expands Espionage Campaign Across Asia with Fake Tax Audits

threat_actors.name: Silver Fox
threat_actors.description: A China-aligned threat group active since at least 2022, conducting dual-purpose espionage and financially motivated campaigns. The group has expanded its targeting from within China to include Taiwan, Japan, and several Southeast Asian countries, with a recent focus on medical and financial sectors.
threat_actors.goals: Data exfiltration for espionage and financial theft.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Used as the primary initial access vector, delivering malicious LNK files or macro-enabled Office documents in emails disguised as official communications like tax audit notices.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The attack relies on the user being tricked into opening a malicious attachment, such as a disguised LNK file or Office document.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Used as part of the execution chain to download and run second-stage payloads after the user opens the initial malicious attachment.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Used to download second-stage payloads from legitimate cloud storage services to evade network-based detection.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The installed remote management tool or custom backdoor likely uses standard HTTP/S for command and control communications to blend in with normal network traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a common method for the installed backdoor to establish persistence on a victim's system.

tools.name: mshta.exe
tools.description: Used to execute remote HTA scripts downloaded as part of the attack chain.

identities.name: Taiwan National Tax Bureau
identities.description: Impersonated by the Silver Fox group in phishing emails sent as fake tax audit notifications to lend legitimacy to their social engineering lures.

infrastructure.name: Payload Delivery Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The attackers used legitimate cloud storage services to host and deliver second-stage payloads, helping to evade network detection.

infrastructure.name: Silver Fox C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the installed backdoor or remote management tool, likely communicating over standard web protocols (HTTP/S).

indicators.name: Suspicious LNK File Attachment
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious LNK File Attachment
id: 7e5f8b9a-1c2d-4e3f-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of .lnk files in common email client or browser download directories, which could indicate a phishing attempt by groups like Silver Fox.
references:
  - https://www.cyberssecuritynews.com/new-silver-fox-campaign/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1566.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.lnk'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects LNK files delivered via email, a technique used by Silver Fox to initiate their attack chain.

indicators.name: MSHTA Remote Script Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: MSHTA Remote Script Execution
id: 8f6a9c8b-2d3e-4f5a-9b8c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: 'Detects mshta.exe making a network connection to download and execute a remote HTA payload, a technique used by groups like Silver Fox.'
references:
  - https://www.cyberssecuritynews.com/new-silver-fox-campaign/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1105
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of mshta.exe to execute remote scripts, a hunting hint provided in the article.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: 9a7b8d9c-3e4f-5a6b-8c7d-2e3f4a5b6c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with an encoded command, a common obfuscation technique used by threat actors like Silver Fox to hide malicious scripts.
references:
  - https://www.cyberssecuritynews.com/new-silver-fox-campaign/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell being launched with encoded commands, a technique used by Silver Fox for obfuscation.

indicators.name: Suspicious Tax Audit Phishing Email
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Tax Audit Phishing Email
id: a1b2c3d4-4f5a-6b7c-9d8e-3f4a5b6c7d8e
status: experimental
description: Detects incoming emails with subjects related to tax audits containing attachments, a lure used by the Silver Fox APT.
references:
  - https://www.cyberssecuritynews.com/new-silver-fox-campaign/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-29
tags:
  - attack.t1566
level: medium
logsource:
  category: email
detection:
  selection:
    Subject|contains: 'tax audit'
    Attachments: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects emails with subjects related to 'tax audit' containing attachments, matching the social engineering theme of the Silver Fox campaign.
